revision.comment: 「栗原神社 (座間市)」立ち上げました。修正・補足などあれば宜しく。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 栗原神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 神奈川県座間市栗原中央4-4-4 | 緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 28 |緯度秒 = 46.82 | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 24 |経度秒 = 53.97 |祭神 = 豊受大神
天御柱命
国御柱命
道反大神
稚日留女命 |神体 = |社格 = 村社 |創建 = [[天正]]元年（[[1573年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 王子大権現 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月上旬 |神事 = }} '''栗原神社'''（くりはらじんじゃ）は[[神奈川県]][[座間市]]にある神社。 == 由緒 == 中栗原の豪農・大矢家の先祖が[[天正]]元年（[[1573年]]）に[[八王子権現|王子大権現]]を勧請して祀ったのが始まりとされる{{citeweb|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=640&cd=1207057&npg=0 |title=栗原神社 |publisher=神奈川県神社庁 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}。以来、'''王子権現社'''として地域の信仰を集めていたが、[[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）に地域の[[絹張社]]・[[握財社]]・[[山王社]]・[[若宮社]]・[[龍蔵社]]が合祀されるとともに栗原地区の総鎮守となって、栗原神社と名を改めた。 === 近代 === 江戸時代からこの地域には祭囃子として下町囃子が伝えられていたが、[[1902年]]（明治35年）頃になって栗原地区に現在の[[綾瀬市]]早川から新たな祭囃子が導入された{{citeweb|url=http://www.nponia.com/page14-maturibayasi%28zama%29.htm |title=「祭囃子（かまくら・やたい）」座間市指定無形民俗文化財 |publisher=NPO法人無形民俗文化財アーカイブズ |date=2009-11-06 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}。これは現在まで伝わって、毎年9月の例祭で披露されており、[[1968年]]（昭和43年）市指定重要文化財に指定された{{citeweb|url=http://www.city.zama.kanagawa.jp/www/contents/1219899063356/index.html |title=市指定重要文化財(天然記念物、無形文化財) |publisher=座間市 |date=2011-05-17 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}。また、境内にある[[シラカシ]]は樹齢500年前後と言われる大木で、[[1978年]]（昭和53年）に市の天然記念物に指定されている。 [[2013年]]（[[平成]]25年）3月7日未明、放火によって社務所や神楽殿が全焼し、神社の由来を記した古文書「栗原寺社村鏡」の写本なども焼失した{{citeweb|url=http://news.kanaloco.jp/localnews/article/1303090006/ |title=座間放火事件：神社神楽殿全焼も関与か、地元から憤りの声 |publisher=神奈川新聞社 |date=2013-03-09 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}。 == 祭神 == * [[トヨウケビメ|豊受大神]] * [[シナツヒコ|天御柱命]] * [[稚日女尊|稚日留女命]] * 道反大神 * 国御柱命 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くりはらしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:座間市]] {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=神奈川県}}

道反大神
稚日留女命 |神体 = |社格 = 村社 |創建 = [[天正]]元年（[[1573年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 王子大権現 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月上旬 |神事 = }} '''栗原神社'''（くりはらじんじゃ）は[[神奈川県]][[座間市]]にある神社。 == 由緒 == 中栗原の豪農・大矢家の先祖が[[天正]]元年（[[1573年]]）に[[八王子権現|王子大権現]]を勧請して祀ったのが始まりとされる{{citeweb|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=640&cd=1207057&npg=0 |title=栗原神社 |publisher=神奈川県神社庁 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}。以来、'''王子権現社'''{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 栗原村 王子権現社}}として地域の信仰を集めていたが、[[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）に地域の[[絹張社]]・[[握財社]]・[[山王社]]・[[若宮社]]・[[龍蔵社]]が合祀されるとともに栗原地区の総鎮守となって、栗原神社と名を改めた。 === 近代 === 江戸時代からこの地域には祭囃子として下町囃子が伝えられていたが、[[1902年]]（明治35年）頃になって栗原地区に現在の[[綾瀬市]]早川から新たな祭囃子が導入された{{citeweb|url=http://www.nponia.com/page14-maturibayasi%28zama%29.htm |title=「祭囃子（かまくら・やたい）」座間市指定無形民俗文化財 |publisher=NPO法人無形民俗文化財アーカイブズ |date=2009-11-06 |accessdate=2013-12-16|deadlink=2021-11}}。これは現在まで伝わって、毎年9月の例祭で披露されており、[[1968年]]（昭和43年）市指定重要文化財に指定された{{citeweb|url=http://www.city.zama.kanagawa.jp/www/contents/1219899063356/index.html |title=市指定重要文化財(天然記念物、無形文化財) |publisher=座間市 |date=2011-05-17 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}。また、境内にある[[シラカシ]]は樹齢500年前後と言われる大木で、[[1978年]]（昭和53年）に市の天然記念物に指定されている。 [[2013年]]（[[平成]]25年）3月7日未明、放火によって社務所や神楽殿が全焼し、神社の由来を記した古文書「栗原寺社村鏡」の写本なども焼失した{{citeweb|url=http://news.kanaloco.jp/localnews/article/1303090006/ |title=座間放火事件：神社神楽殿全焼も関与か、地元から憤りの声 |publisher=神奈川新聞社 |date=2013-03-09 |accessdate=2013-12-16|deadlink=2021-11}}。 == 祭神 == * [[トヨウケビメ|豊受大神]] * [[シナツヒコ|天御柱命]] * [[稚日女尊|稚日留女命]] * 道反大神 * 国御柱命 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第38巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]3巻之66村里部高座郡巻之8|chapter=澁谷庄 栗原村 王子権現社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/179}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 栗原村 王子権現社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くりはらしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:座間市の歴史]] [[Category:座間市の建築物]]

[[国御柱命]]
道反大神
稚日留女命 |神体 = |社格 = 村社 |創建 = [[天正]]元年（[[1573年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 王子大権現 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月上旬 |神事 = }} [[File:Kurihara Shrine in city at Zama gate.jpg|240px|thumb|栗原神社鳥居]] '''栗原神社'''（くりはらじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[座間市]]栗原中央にある神社。 == 由緒 == 中栗原の豪農・大矢家の先祖が[[天正]]元年（[[1573年]]）に[[八王子権現|王子大権現]]を勧請して祀ったのが始まりとされる{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=640&cd=1207057&npg=0 |title=栗原神社 |publisher=神奈川県神社庁 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}。以来、'''王子権現社'''{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 栗原村 王子権現社}}として地域の信仰を集めていたが、[[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）に地域の[[絹張社]]・[[握財社]]・[[山王社]]・[[若宮社]]・[[龍蔵社]]が合祀されるとともに栗原地区の総鎮守となって、栗原神社と名を改めた。 === 近代 === 江戸時代からこの地域には祭囃子として下町囃子が伝えられていたが、[[1902年]]（明治35年）頃になって栗原地区に現在の[[綾瀬市]]早川から新たな祭囃子が導入された{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nponia.com/page14-maturibayasi%28zama%29.htm |title=「祭囃子（かまくら・やたい）」座間市指定無形民俗文化財 |publisher=NPO法人無形民俗文化財アーカイブズ |date=2009-11-06 |accessdate=2013-12-16|url-status=dead|url-status-date=2021-11}}。これは現在まで伝わって、毎年9月の例祭で披露されており、[[1968年]]（昭和43年）市指定重要文化財に指定された{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.zama.kanagawa.jp/www/contents/1219899063356/index.html |title=市指定重要文化財(天然記念物、無形文化財) |publisher=座間市 |date=2011-05-17 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}。また、境内にある[[シラカシ]]は樹齢500年前後と言われる大木で、[[1978年]]（昭和53年）に市の天然記念物に指定されている。 [[2013年]]（[[平成]]25年）3月7日未明、放火によって社務所や神楽殿が全焼し、神社の由来を記した古文書「栗原寺社村鏡」の写本なども焼失した{{Cite web|和書|url=http://news.kanaloco.jp/localnews/article/1303090006/ |title=座間放火事件：神社神楽殿全焼も関与か、地元から憤りの声 |publisher=神奈川新聞社 |date=2013-03-09 |accessdate=2013-12-16|url-status=dead|url-status-date=2021-11}}。 == 祭神 == * [[トヨウケビメ|豊受大神]] * [[シナツヒコ|天御柱命]] * [[稚日女尊|稚日留女命]] * 道反大神 * 国御柱命 {{-}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第38巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]3巻之66村里部高座郡巻之8|chapter=澁谷庄 栗原村 王子権現社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/179}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 栗原村 王子権現社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くりはらしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:座間市の歴史]] [[Category:座間市の建築物]]

'''Kurihara Shrine''' (栗原神社, くりはらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Zama, Kanagawa|Zama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==

The shrine was established in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 1 (1573) when the ancestors of the Ōya family, a prominent landowning family in Nakakurihara, enshrined the [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Gongen]] deity.<ref name="jinja">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=640&cd=1207057&npg=0 |title=栗原神社 |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> Originally known as '''Ōji Gongen Shrine''', it was a focal point of local worship. In 1873 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 6), several neighboring shrines, including Kinu Harisha, Nigusai Shrine, Sannō Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, and Ryūzō Shrine, were merged into Kurihara Shrine, which became the central guardian shrine of the Kurihara area. At this time, the shrine was renamed Kurihara Shrine.<ref name="jinja"/>

=== Modern Period ===
Since the Edo period, the local festival music called "Shimomachi-bayashi" had been performed in the area. Around 1902 (Meiji 35), a new style of festival music was introduced from Hayakawa, Ayase City, into the Kurihara region.<ref name="hayashi">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nponia.com/page14-maturibayasi%28zama%29.htm |title=「祭囃子（かまくら・やたい）」座間市指定無形民俗文化財 |publisher=NPO Archives for Intangible Cultural Heritage |date=2009-11-06 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> This music is still performed annually at the September festival and was designated an Important Cultural Property by Zama City in 1968 (Shōwa 43).<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.zama.kanagawa.jp/www/contents/1219899063356/index.html |title=市指定重要文化財(天然記念物、無形文化財) |publisher=Zama City |date=2011-05-17 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>

A notable feature of the shrine's precincts is a large [[:en:Quercus myrsinifolia|Shirakashi]] tree, estimated to be over 500 years old. This tree was designated a Natural Monument by Zama City in 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="bunka"/>

On March 7, 2013 (Heisei 25), a fire believed to be arson destroyed the shrine office and the kaguraden (sacred stage). Historical documents such as a copy of the "Kurihara Shrine Mirror" were also lost in the blaze.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://news.kanaloco.jp/localnews/article/1303090006/ |title=座間放火事件：神社神楽殿全焼も関与か、地元から憤りの声 |publisher=Kanagawa Newspaper |date=2013-03-09 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>
[[ja:栗原神社 (座間市)]]

== Deities ==
The shrine enshrines the following deities:
* {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}

* {{ill|Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja|稚日留女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahirume no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja|道反大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michikaeshi no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|ja|国御柱命|lt=Kuni no Mihashira no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}

'''Kurihara Shrine''' (栗原神社, くりはらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Zama, Kanagawa|Zama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==

The shrine was established in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 1 (1573) when the ancestors of the Ōya family, a prominent landowning family in Nakakurihara, enshrined the [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Gongen]] deity.<ref name="jinja">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=640&cd=1207057&npg=0 |title=栗原神社 |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> Originally known as '''Ōji Gongen Shrine''', it was a focal point of local worship. In 1873 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 6), several neighboring shrines, including Kinu Harisha, Nigusai Shrine, Sannō Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, and Ryūzō Shrine, were merged into Kurihara Shrine, which became the central guardian shrine of the Kurihara area. At this time, the shrine was renamed Kurihara Shrine.<ref name="jinja"/>

=== Modern Period ===
Since the Edo period, the local festival music called "Shimomachi-bayashi" had been performed in the area. Around 1902 (Meiji 35), a new style of festival music was introduced from Hayakawa, Ayase City, into the Kurihara region.<ref name="hayashi">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nponia.com/page14-maturibayasi%28zama%29.htm |title=「祭囃子（かまくら・やたい）」座間市指定無形民俗文化財 |publisher=NPO Archives for Intangible Cultural Heritage |date=2009-11-06 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> This music is still performed annually at the September festival and was designated an Important Cultural Property by Zama City in 1968 (Shōwa 43).<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.zama.kanagawa.jp/www/contents/1219899063356/index.html |title=市指定重要文化財(天然記念物、無形文化財) |publisher=Zama City |date=2011-05-17 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>

A notable feature of the shrine's precincts is a large [[:en:Quercus myrsinifolia|Shirakashi]] tree, estimated to be over 500 years old. This tree was designated a Natural Monument by Zama City in 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="bunka"/>

On March 7, 2013 (Heisei 25), a fire believed to be arson destroyed the shrine office and the kaguraden (sacred stage). Historical documents such as a copy of the "Kurihara Shrine Mirror" were also lost in the blaze.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://news.kanaloco.jp/localnews/article/1303090006/ |title=座間放火事件：神社神楽殿全焼も関与か、地元から憤りの声 |publisher=Kanagawa Newspaper |date=2013-03-09 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>
[[ja:栗原神社 (座間市)]]

== Deities ==
The shrine enshrines the following deities:
* {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Ame no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja|天御柱命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}
* {{ill|Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja|稚日留女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja|道反大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|ja|国御柱命|lt=Kuni no Mihashira no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}

'''Kurihara Shrine''' (栗原神社, くりはらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Zama, Kanagawa|Zama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==

The shrine was established in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 1 (1573) when the ancestors of the Ōya family, a prominent landowning family in Nakakurihara, enshrined the [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Gongen]] deity.<ref name="jinja">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=640&cd=1207057&npg=0 |title=栗原神社 |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> Originally known as '''Ōji Gongen Shrine''', it was a focal point of local worship. In 1873 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 6), several neighboring shrines, including Kinu Harisha, Nigusai Shrine, Sannō Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, and Ryūzō Shrine, were merged into Kurihara Shrine, which became the central guardian shrine of the Kurihara area. At this time, the shrine was renamed Kurihara Shrine.<ref name="jinja"/>

=== Modern Period ===
Since the Edo period, the local festival music called "Shimomachi-bayashi" had been performed in the area. Around 1902 (Meiji 35), a new style of festival music was introduced from Hayakawa, Ayase City, into the Kurihara region.<ref name="hayashi">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nponia.com/page14-maturibayasi%28zama%29.htm |title=「祭囃子（かまくら・やたい）」座間市指定無形民俗文化財 |publisher=NPO Archives for Intangible Cultural Heritage |date=2009-11-06 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> This music is still performed annually at the September festival and was designated an Important Cultural Property by Zama City in 1968 (Shōwa 43).<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.zama.kanagawa.jp/www/contents/1219899063356/index.html |title=市指定重要文化財(天然記念物、無形文化財) |publisher=Zama City |date=2011-05-17 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>

A notable feature of the shrine's precincts is a large [[:en:Quercus myrsinifolia|Shirakashi]] tree, estimated to be over 500 years old. This tree was designated a Natural Monument by Zama City in 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="bunka"/>

On March 7, 2013 (Heisei 25), a fire believed to be arson destroyed the shrine office and the kaguraden (sacred stage). Historical documents such as a copy of the "Kurihara Shrine Mirror" were also lost in the blaze.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://news.kanaloco.jp/localnews/article/1303090006/ |title=座間放火事件：神社神楽殿全焼も関与か、地元から憤りの声 |publisher=Kanagawa Newspaper |date=2013-03-09 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>
[[ja:栗原神社 (座間市)]]

== Deities ==
The shrine enshrines the following deities:
* {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Ame no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja|天御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}
* {{ill|Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja|稚日留女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja|道反大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|ja|国御柱命|lt=Kuni no Mihashira no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}

'''Kurihara Shrine''' (栗原神社, くりはらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Zama, Kanagawa|Zama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==

The shrine was established in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 1 (1573) when the ancestors of the Ōya family, a prominent landowning family in Nakakurihara, enshrined the [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Gongen]] deity.<ref name="jinja">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=640&cd=1207057&npg=0 |title=栗原神社 |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> Originally known as '''Ōji Gongen Shrine''', it was a focal point of local worship. In 1873 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 6), several neighboring shrines, including Kinu Harisha, Nigusai Shrine, Sannō Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, and Ryūzō Shrine, were merged into Kurihara Shrine, which became the central guardian shrine of the Kurihara area. At this time, the shrine was renamed Kurihara Shrine.<ref name="jinja"/>

=== Modern Period ===
Since the Edo period, the local festival music called "Shimomachi-bayashi" had been performed in the area. Around 1902 (Meiji 35), a new style of festival music was introduced from Hayakawa, Ayase City, into the Kurihara region.<ref name="hayashi">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nponia.com/page14-maturibayasi%28zama%29.htm |title=「祭囃子（かまくら・やたい）」座間市指定無形民俗文化財 |publisher=NPO Archives for Intangible Cultural Heritage |date=2009-11-06 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> This music is still performed annually at the September festival and was designated an Important Cultural Property by Zama City in 1968 (Shōwa 43).<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.zama.kanagawa.jp/www/contents/1219899063356/index.html |title=市指定重要文化財(天然記念物、無形文化財) |publisher=Zama City |date=2011-05-17 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>

A notable feature of the shrine's precincts is a large [[:en:Quercus myrsinifolia|Shirakashi]] tree, estimated to be over 500 years old. This tree was designated a Natural Monument by Zama City in 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="bunka"/>

On March 7, 2013 (Heisei 25), a fire believed to be arson destroyed the shrine office and the kaguraden (sacred stage). Historical documents such as a copy of the "Kurihara Shrine Mirror" were also lost in the blaze.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://news.kanaloco.jp/localnews/article/1303090006/ |title=座間放火事件：神社神楽殿全焼も関与か、地元から憤りの声 |publisher=Kanagawa Newspaper |date=2013-03-09 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>
[[ja:栗原神社 (座間市)]]

== Deities ==
The shrine enshrines the following deities:
* {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Ame no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja|天御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}
* {{ill|Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja|稚日留女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja|道反大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|ja|国御柱命|lt=Kuni no Mihashira no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}

|ShrineRank={{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}

'''Kurihara Shrine''' (栗原神社, くりはらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Zama, Kanagawa|Zama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==

The shrine was established in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 1 (1573) when the ancestors of the Ōya family, a prominent landowning family in Nakakurihara, enshrined the [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Gongen]] deity.<ref name="jinja">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=640&cd=1207057&npg=0 |title=栗原神社 |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> Originally known as '''Ōji Gongen Shrine''', it was a focal point of local worship. In 1873 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 6), several neighboring shrines, including Kinu Harisha, Nigusai Shrine, Sannō Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, and Ryūzō Shrine, were merged into Kurihara Shrine, which became the central guardian shrine of the Kurihara area. At this time, the shrine was renamed Kurihara Shrine.<ref name="jinja"/>

=== Modern Period ===
Since the Edo period, the local festival music called "Shimomachi-bayashi" had been performed in the area. Around 1902 (Meiji 35), a new style of festival music was introduced from Hayakawa, Ayase City, into the Kurihara region.<ref name="hayashi">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nponia.com/page14-maturibayasi%28zama%29.htm |title=「祭囃子（かまくら・やたい）」座間市指定無形民俗文化財 |publisher=NPO Archives for Intangible Cultural Heritage |date=2009-11-06 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> This music is still performed annually at the September festival and was designated an Important Cultural Property by Zama City in 1968 (Shōwa 43).<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.zama.kanagawa.jp/www/contents/1219899063356/index.html |title=市指定重要文化財(天然記念物、無形文化財) |publisher=Zama City |date=2011-05-17 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>

A notable feature of the shrine's precincts is a large [[:en:Quercus myrsinifolia|Shirakashi]] tree, estimated to be over 500 years old. This tree was designated a Natural Monument by Zama City in 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="bunka"/>

On March 7, 2013 (Heisei 25), a fire believed to be arson destroyed the shrine office and the kaguraden (sacred stage). Historical documents such as a copy of the "Kurihara Shrine Mirror" were also lost in the blaze.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://news.kanaloco.jp/localnews/article/1303090006/ |title=座間放火事件：神社神楽殿全焼も関与か、地元から憤りの声 |publisher=Kanagawa Newspaper |date=2013-03-09 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>
[[ja:栗原神社 (座間市)]]

== Deities ==
The shrine enshrines the following deities:
* {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Ame no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja|天御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}
* {{ill|Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja|稚日留女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja|道反大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|ja|国御柱命|lt=Kuni no Mihashira no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}

== Notes ==

== References ==

|ShrineRank={{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
|consecration_year=[[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 1 (1573)
|prefecture={{ill|Kurihara-chūō|WD=Q121494610}}; {{ill|Zama|WD=Q145333}}

'''Kurihara Shrine''' (栗原神社, くりはらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Zama, Kanagawa|Zama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==

The shrine was established in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 1 (1573) when the ancestors of the Ōya family, a prominent landowning family in Nakakurihara, enshrined the [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Gongen]] deity.<ref name="jinja">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=640&cd=1207057&npg=0 |title=栗原神社 |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> Originally known as '''Ōji Gongen Shrine''', it was a focal point of local worship. In 1873 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 6), several neighboring shrines, including Kinu Harisha, Nigusai Shrine, Sannō Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, and Ryūzō Shrine, were merged into Kurihara Shrine, which became the central guardian shrine of the Kurihara area. At this time, the shrine was renamed Kurihara Shrine.<ref name="jinja"/>

=== Modern Period ===
Since the Edo period, the local festival music called "Shimomachi-bayashi" had been performed in the area. Around 1902 (Meiji 35), a new style of festival music was introduced from Hayakawa, Ayase City, into the Kurihara region.<ref name="hayashi">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nponia.com/page14-maturibayasi%28zama%29.htm |title=「祭囃子（かまくら・やたい）」座間市指定無形民俗文化財 |publisher=NPO Archives for Intangible Cultural Heritage |date=2009-11-06 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> This music is still performed annually at the September festival and was designated an Important Cultural Property by Zama City in 1968 (Shōwa 43).<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.zama.kanagawa.jp/www/contents/1219899063356/index.html |title=市指定重要文化財(天然記念物、無形文化財) |publisher=Zama City |date=2011-05-17 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>

A notable feature of the shrine's precincts is a large [[:en:Quercus myrsinifolia|Shirakashi]] tree, estimated to be over 500 years old. This tree was designated a Natural Monument by Zama City in 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="bunka"/>

On March 7, 2013 (Heisei 25), a fire believed to be arson destroyed the shrine office and the kaguraden (sacred stage). Historical documents such as a copy of the "Kurihara Shrine Mirror" were also lost in the blaze.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://news.kanaloco.jp/localnews/article/1303090006/ |title=座間放火事件：神社神楽殿全焼も関与か、地元から憤りの声 |publisher=Kanagawa Newspaper |date=2013-03-09 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>
[[ja:栗原神社 (座間市)]]

== Deities ==
The shrine enshrines the following deities:
* {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Ame no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja|天御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}
* {{ill|Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja|稚日留女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja|道反大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|ja|国御柱命|lt=Kuni no Mihashira no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}

|deity={{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, {{ill|Ame no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja|天御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}, {{ill|Kuni no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja|国御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}, {{ill|Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja|道反大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja|稚日留女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
|ShrineRank={{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
|consecration_year=[[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 1 (1573)
|prefecture={{ill|Kurihara-chūō|WD=Q121494610}}; {{ill|Zama|WD=Q145333}}
}}
'''Kurihara Shrine''' (栗原神社, くりはらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Zama, Kanagawa|Zama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==

The shrine was established in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 1 (1573) when the ancestors of the Ōya family, a prominent landowning family in Nakakurihara, enshrined the [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Gongen]] deity.<ref name="jinja">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=640&cd=1207057&npg=0 |title=栗原神社 |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> Originally known as '''Ōji Gongen Shrine''', it was a focal point of local worship. In 1873 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 6), several neighboring shrines, including Kinu Harisha, Nigusai Shrine, Sannō Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, and Ryūzō Shrine, were merged into Kurihara Shrine, which became the central guardian shrine of the Kurihara area. At this time, the shrine was renamed Kurihara Shrine.<ref name="jinja"/>

=== Modern Period ===
Since the Edo period, the local festival music called "Shimomachi-bayashi" had been performed in the area. Around 1902 (Meiji 35), a new style of festival music was introduced from Hayakawa, Ayase City, into the Kurihara region.<ref name="hayashi">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nponia.com/page14-maturibayasi%28zama%29.htm |title=「祭囃子（かまくら・やたい）」座間市指定無形民俗文化財 |publisher=NPO Archives for Intangible Cultural Heritage |date=2009-11-06 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> This music is still performed annually at the September festival and was designated an Important Cultural Property by Zama City in 1968 (Shōwa 43).<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.zama.kanagawa.jp/www/contents/1219899063356/index.html |title=市指定重要文化財(天然記念物、無形文化財) |publisher=Zama City |date=2011-05-17 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>

A notable feature of the shrine's precincts is a large [[:en:Quercus myrsinifolia|Shirakashi]] tree, estimated to be over 500 years old. This tree was designated a Natural Monument by Zama City in 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="bunka"/>

On March 7, 2013 (Heisei 25), a fire believed to be arson destroyed the shrine office and the kaguraden (sacred stage). Historical documents such as a copy of the "Kurihara Shrine Mirror" were also lost in the blaze.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://news.kanaloco.jp/localnews/article/1303090006/ |title=座間放火事件：神社神楽殿全焼も関与か、地元から憤りの声 |publisher=Kanagawa Newspaper |date=2013-03-09 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>
[[ja:栗原神社 (座間市)]]

== Deities ==
The shrine enshrines the following deities:
* {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Ame no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja|天御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}
* {{ill|Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja|稚日留女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja|道反大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|ja|国御柱命|lt=Kuni no Mihashira no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}

| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]

|location={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Zama, Kanagawa|en|Zama, Kanagawa|WD=Q145333|lt=Zama City}} Kurihara-Chūō 4-4-4
|coordinates={{Coord|35.47967222222222|N|139.41499166666668|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|deity={{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, {{ill|Ame no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja|天御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}, {{ill|Kuni no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja|国御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}, {{ill|Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja|道反大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja|稚日留女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
|ShrineRank={{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
|consecration_year={{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 1 (1573)
|prefecture={{ill|Kurihara-chūō|WD=Q121494610}}; {{ill|Zama|WD=Q145333}}
}}
'''Kurihara Shrine''' (栗原神社, くりはらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Zama, Kanagawa|en|Zama, Kanagawa|WD=Q145333|lt=Zama City}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, Japan.

== History ==

The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 1 (1573) when the ancestors of the Ōya family, a prominent landowning family in Nakakurihara, enshrined the {{ill|Ōji Shrine|en|Ōji Shrine|WD=Q11573004|lt=Ōji Gongen}} deity.<ref name="jinja">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=640&cd=1207057&npg=0 |title=栗原神社 |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> Originally known as '''Ōji Gongen Shrine''', it was a focal point of local worship. In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 6), several neighboring shrines, including Kinu Harisha, Nigusai Shrine, Sannō Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, and Ryūzō Shrine, were merged into Kurihara Shrine, which became the central guardian shrine of the Kurihara area. At this time, the shrine was renamed Kurihara Shrine.<ref name="jinja"/>

=== Modern Period ===
Since the Edo period, the local festival music called "Shimomachi-bayashi" had been performed in the area. Around 1902 (Meiji 35), a new style of festival music was introduced from Hayakawa, Ayase City, into the Kurihara region.<ref name="hayashi">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nponia.com/page14-maturibayasi%28zama%29.htm |title=「祭囃子（かまくら・やたい）」座間市指定無形民俗文化財 |publisher=NPO Archives for Intangible Cultural Heritage |date=2009-11-06 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> This music is still performed annually at the September festival and was designated an Important Cultural Property by Zama City in 1968 (Shōwa 43).<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.zama.kanagawa.jp/www/contents/1219899063356/index.html |title=市指定重要文化財(天然記念物、無形文化財) |publisher=Zama City |date=2011-05-17 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>

A notable feature of the shrine's precincts is a large {{ill|Quercus myrsinifolia|en|Quercus myrsinifolia|WD=Q1075985|lt=Shirakashi}} tree, estimated to be over 500 years old. This tree was designated a Natural Monument by Zama City in 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="bunka"/>

On March 7, 2013 (Heisei 25), a fire believed to be arson destroyed the shrine office and the kaguraden (sacred stage). Historical documents such as a copy of the "Kurihara Shrine Mirror" were also lost in the blaze.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://news.kanaloco.jp/localnews/article/1303090006/ |title=座間放火事件：神社神楽殿全焼も関与か、地元から憤りの声 |publisher=Kanagawa Newspaper |date=2013-03-09 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>
[[ja:栗原神社 (座間市)]]

== Deities ==
The shrine enshrines the following deities:
* {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Ame no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja|天御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}
* {{ill|Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja|稚日留女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja|道反大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|ja|国御柱命|lt=Kuni no Mihashira no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}

Historical documents such as a copy of the "Kurihara Shrine Mirror" were also lost in the blaze.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://news.kanaloco.jp/localnews/article/1303090006/ |title=座間放火事件：神社神楽殿全焼も関与か、地元から憤りの声 |publisher=Kanagawa Newspaper |date=2013-03-09 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>
[[ja:栗原神社 (座間市)]]

== Deities ==
The shrine enshrines the following deities:
* {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}

* {{ill|Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja|道反大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

|consecration_year={{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 1 (1573)
|prefecture={{ill|Kurihara-chūō|WD=Q121494610|1=Kurihara-chūō}}; {{ill|Zama|WD=Q145333|1=Zama, Kanagawa}}

'''Kurihara Shrine''' (栗原神社, くりはらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Zama, Kanagawa|en|Zama, Kanagawa|WD=Q145333|lt=Zama City|1=Zama, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, Japan.

== History ==

The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 1 (1573) when the ancestors of the Ōya family, a prominent landowning family in Nakakurihara, enshrined the {{ill|Ōji Shrine|en|Ōji Shrine|WD=Q11573004|lt=Ōji Gongen|1=Ōji Shrine}} deity.<ref name="jinja">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=640&cd=1207057&npg=0 |title=栗原神社 |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> Originally known as '''Ōji Gongen Shrine''', it was a focal point of local worship. In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 6), several neighboring shrines, including Kinu Harisha, Nigusai Shrine, Sannō Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, and Ryūzō Shrine, were merged into Kurihara Shrine, which became the central guardian shrine of the Kurihara area. At this time, the shrine was renamed Kurihara Shrine.<ref name="jinja"/>

=== Modern Period ===
Since the Edo period, the local festival music called "Shimomachi-bayashi" had been performed in the area. Around 1902 (Meiji 35), a new style of festival music was introduced from Hayakawa, Ayase City, into the Kurihara region.<ref name="hayashi">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nponia.com/page14-maturibayasi%28zama%29.htm |title=「祭囃子（かまくら・やたい）」座間市指定無形民俗文化財 |publisher=NPO Archives for Intangible Cultural Heritage |date=2009-11-06 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> This music is still performed annually at the September festival and was designated an Important Cultural Property by Zama City in 1968 (Shōwa 43).<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.zama.kanagawa.jp/www/contents/1219899063356/index.html |title=市指定重要文化財(天然記念物、無形文化財) |publisher=Zama City |date=2011-05-17 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>

A notable feature of the shrine's precincts is a large {{ill|Quercus myrsinifolia|en|Quercus myrsinifolia|WD=Q1075985|lt=Shirakashi|1=Quercus myrsinifolia}} tree, estimated to be over 500 years old. This tree was designated a Natural Monument by Zama City in 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="bunka"/>

On March 7, 2013 (Heisei 25), a fire believed to be arson destroyed the shrine office and the kaguraden (sacred stage). Historical documents such as a copy of the "Kurihara Shrine Mirror" were also lost in the blaze.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://news.kanaloco.jp/localnews/article/1303090006/ |title=座間放火事件：神社神楽殿全焼も関与か、地元から憤りの声 |publisher=Kanagawa Newspaper |date=2013-03-09 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>
[[ja:栗原神社 (座間市)]]

== Deities ==
The shrine enshrines the following deities:
* {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Ame no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja|天御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}
* {{ill|Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja|稚日留女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja|道反大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|ja|国御柱命|lt=Kuni no Mihashira no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}

'''Kurihara Shrine''' (栗原神社, くりはらじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Zama, Kanagawa|en|Zama, Kanagawa|WD=Q145333|lt=Zama City|1=Zama, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, Japan.

== History ==

The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 1 (1573) when the ancestors of the Ōya family, a prominent landowning family in Nakakurihara, enshrined the {{ill|Ōji Shrine|en|Ōji Shrine|WD=Q11573004|lt=Ōji Gongen|1=Ōji Shrine}} deity.<ref name="jinja">{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=640&cd=1207057&npg=0 |title=栗原神社 |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> Originally known as '''Ōji Gongen Shrine''', it was a focal point of local worship. In 1873 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 6), several neighboring shrines, including Kinu Harisha, Nigusai Shrine, Sannō Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, and Ryūzō Shrine, were merged into Kurihara Shrine, which became the central guardian shrine of the Kurihara area. At this time, the shrine was renamed Kurihara Shrine.<ref name="jinja"/>

=== Modern Period ===
Since the Edo period, the local festival music called "Shimomachi-bayashi" had been performed in the area. Around 1902 (Meiji 35), a new style of festival music was introduced from Hayakawa, Ayase City, into the Kurihara region.<ref name="hayashi">{{Cite web|url=http://www.nponia.com/page14-maturibayasi%28zama%29.htm |title=「祭囃子（かまくら・やたい）」座間市指定無形民俗文化財 |publisher=NPO Archives for Intangible Cultural Heritage |date=2009-11-06 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref> This music is still performed annually at the September festival and was designated an Important Cultural Property by Zama City in 1968 (Shōwa 43).<ref name="bunka">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.zama.kanagawa.jp/www/contents/1219899063356/index.html |title=市指定重要文化財(天然記念物、無形文化財) |publisher=Zama City |date=2011-05-17 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>

A notable feature of the shrine's precincts is a large {{ill|Quercus myrsinifolia|en|Quercus myrsinifolia|WD=Q1075985|lt=Shirakashi|1=Quercus myrsinifolia}} tree, estimated to be over 500 years old. This tree was designated a Natural Monument by Zama City in 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="bunka"/>

On March 7, 2013 (Heisei 25), a fire believed to be arson destroyed the shrine office and the kaguraden (sacred stage). Historical documents such as a copy of the "Kurihara Shrine Mirror" were also lost in the blaze.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://news.kanaloco.jp/localnews/article/1303090006/ |title=座間放火事件：神社神楽殿全焼も関与か、地元から憤りの声 |publisher=Kanagawa Newspaper |date=2013-03-09 |accessdate=2013-12-16}}</ref>
[[ja:栗原神社 (座間市)]]

== Deities ==
The shrine enshrines the following deities:
* {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Ame no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja|天御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}
* {{ill|Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja|稚日留女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahirume no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja|道反大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michikaeshi no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|ja|国御柱命|lt=Kuni no Mihashira no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}
